Sunday, June 28, 2026 Daily Digest

JustAI Raises $17M for Coordinated Agents, Enterprise AI Goes Production, Agentic Governance Gap Widens

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

JustAI Raises $17M to Build an Agentic Marketing Platform

Base10 Partners leads a $17M Series A for JustAI, which replaces fragmented martech with four coordinated AI agents: Strategy (audits users and segments), Creative (generates brand-forward content), Decisioning (optimizes for business goals with guardrails), and Data (measures lift and feeds learnings back). Already at 5x ARR growth with $100M+ in customer value delivered. The architecture matters: it's not another point solution — it's an operating system.

Read on PR Newswire →
2

Enterprise AI Moves from Pilot to Production — But Governance Lags

Deloitte's 2026 State of AI in the Enterprise report shows worker AI access up 50%, and companies with 40%+ AI experiments in production set to double in six months. NVIDIA's survey of 3,200+ enterprises found 64% actively using AI in operations. But here's the catch: agentic AI adoption is about to double (from 23% to 46%) — yet only 1 in 5 companies have mature governance models. The scaling era has begun, and oversight can't keep up.

Read on MarketScale →
3

Zeta Global + Palantir Build Agentic Marketing Infrastructure

Zeta will rearchitect its Data Cloud on Palantir Foundry, connecting Zeta's Athena AI engine with enterprise operational data for real-time marketing decisions and automation. CEO David Steinberg says the deal could generate $100M+ annually. The play: combine customer intelligence with Foundry's governance and ontology layer to support agentic marketing applications from acquisition to retention.

Read on MarTech →
4

ActiveCampaign's AI Now Remembers Your Brand Across Sessions

Active Intelligence 2.8 introduces persistent brand memory — the AI now remembers corporate voice guidelines, colors, logos, and strategic priorities across design sessions when drafting marketing assets and automated sequences. It's a small feature with big implications: AI marketing tools are evolving from task executors to brand partners that learn and remember.

Read on MarTech →
5

Hootsuite Launches "Wisdom" — An AI Agent for Social Listening

Hootsuite's new AI agent monitors, filters, and responds to social signals across channels. Wisdom categorizes user intent, tracks brand sentiment, and drafts replies based on past engagement patterns. The social listening space is going agentic — moving from passive dashboards to active agents that can act on signals in real time.

Read on MarTech →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Deloitte/NVIDIA enterprise AI report. The headline stat — 64% of enterprises actively using AI in operations — is impressive. But the governance gap is the real story. Agentic AI adoption is about to double, yet 4 out of 5 companies lack mature oversight. That's the opportunity for enterprise leaders: be the bridge between AI ambition and responsible execution.
